summary: DeVault praises qemu as one of his favorite pieces of software, highlighting its role as the foundation of modern cloud virtualization and its invaluable utility for kernel development. He credits Fabrice Bellard for creating another revolutionary tool, comparable to ffmpeg's impact on multimedia. The post details qemu's comprehensive virtual device support, its power as a kernel debugging environment, and the lesser-known qemu-user feature for cross-architecture development. DeVault notes the clean C implementation and the personal significance of qemu as one of his first email-based open source contributions.
key_points: qemu combined with KVM forms the foundation of nearly all cloud services and modern virtualization
For kernel development, qemu provides an unprecedented luxury of debugging and testing that makes working on kernels nearly as easy as working on regular programs
qemu-user with binfmt handlers enables running executables compiled for arbitrary architectures, making cross-arch development trivially easy
The C implementation is simple, robust, and a pleasure to read
qemu still uses email-based development and even recommends SourceHut to newcomers
money_quotes: Much like Bellard's ffmpeg revolutionized the multimedia software industry, qemu revolutionized virtualisation.
In my reckoning, qemu support is nearly as important for bringing up a new system as a C compiler is.
Anyone who remembers osdev in the Bochs days — or even earlier — understands the unprecedented luxury of such a development environment.

Sure, it's low level — running a qemu command line is certainly more intimidating than, say, VirtualBox — but the trade-off in power afforded to the user opens up innumerable use-cases that are simply not available on any other virtualisation platform.
And qemu's implementation in C is simple, robust, and comprehensive. On the several occasions when I've had to read the code, it has been a pleasure.
If you do cross-architecture work and you haven't set up qemu-user yet, you're missing out.
key_insight: qemu is a quietly revolutionary piece of infrastructure that not only underpins modern cloud computing but provides kernel developers and cross-architecture programmers with development capabilities that would have been unimaginable a generation ago.
